text: Introduction Early diagnosis of brain tumors is important. The classification of brain tumors is carried out by means of biopsy which is done by means of definitive brain surgery. Several computational intelligence-based methods have been employed by the researchers to assist the doctors in identifying and classifying various types of tumors. In this work, two deep learning methods and several machine learning methods have been investigated for the classification of three types of tumors, namely glioma, meningioma, pituitary gland tumors, and healthy brains or brains free from tumors, using MRI brain images. So, it becomes easier for the doctors to detect tumors in early stages with high accuracy. Experimental The experiments were conducted on a dataset of 3264 MRI brain images that contained glioma, meningioma, pituitary gland tumors and healthy or tumor free brains. The MRI brain images were preprocessed and also augmented. A new 2D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and a convolutional auto-encoder network were developed and already trained by our assigned hyperparameters. The 2D CNN architecture has several convolution layers. All layers in this hierarchical network have a 2*2 kernel function. The network has eight convolutional layers and four pooling layers. After all the convolution layers, batch-normalization layers are applied. The modified auto-encoder network is composed of a convolutional auto-encoder network and a convolutional network for classification that uses the last output encoder layer of the first part. Six machine-learning techniques are also employed and compared in this study.
